title: Sealed Bids
premise: At the Marchbanks Estate auction, you and your rivals have submitted sealed bids for Lot 47—a dusty painting that may hide a treasure map. A mysterious bidder seems determined to outspend you, but paying too much could bankrupt your plans. Win the lot without overpaying, or lose it to a rival with deeper pockets.
setting: England, 1925. The grand but decaying Marchbanks Estate is being sold off after the family's fall from fortune. The auction is in the wood-paneled library; bidders mill about, eyeing furniture, books, and curios. Lot 47 is a dark, unremarkable landscape painting, but rumors suggest the late Lord Marchbanks hid a secret beneath the canvas.
goal: Acquire Lot 47 at a price that leaves you with enough resources to act on what it reveals (if anything). Success: win the lot for ≤ your secret limit. Failure: pay more than your limit (winner's curse) and be unable to exploit the secret, or lose the lot entirely.
gm_notes: The mysterious rival is actually Lord Marchbanks' disgraced grandson, who knows the painting conceals a bank vault key. He has a high limit but is bluffing—if the player bids modestly, he might not top it. Introduce a third bidder (the art collector) who can drive up the price if he detects interest. Options for complications: a forged bid, a last-minute letter from the Marchbanks solicitor, or the painting being swapped. Casual conversation among NPCs can reveal clues about the painting's worth or the rival's identity.

roles.name: The Heir
roles.description: A young, cash-strapped relative of the Marchbanks family, here to reclaim a piece of your heritage.
roles.private_info: You know the painting was your Great-Uncle's favorite; he once told you it hides a clue to a family fortune. You have £2,500 in savings and a loan from your mother. Your max bid is £1,800.

roles.name: The Dealer
roles.description: A shrewd antiques dealer with a nose for hidden value. You operate under a business name and have connections.
roles.private_info: You've heard rumors of a treasure map under the painting. You have £5,000 capital from your shop sale, but your max bid is £2,200—any higher would ruin your profit margin.

roles.name: The Widow
roles.description: A mysterious woman in black who claims to be a family friend. She seems to have inside knowledge.
roles.private_info: You were Lord Marchbanks' secret lover. He gave you a letter hinting at a vault key in the painting. You have £1,500 from selling your jewelry; your max bid is £1,200.

npcs.name: Auctioneer Mr. Grimshaw
npcs.description: A portly man in a rumpled suit, gavel in hand. He runs the auction with practiced authority.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He's been paid off by the grandson to overlook any irregularities. He wants to close the auction quickly and avoid scandal.

npcs.name: The Grandson (Rival)
npcs.description: A well-dressed young man with a brusque manner, examining his pocket watch. He's the one driving up bids on Lot 47.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He knows the painting holds a key to a bank vault containing his inheritance. He has only £2,000 of his own, but is willing to bluff—he'll drop out if someone bids over £2,000.

npcs.name: Mrs. Hargrove
npcs.description: An elderly gossipy neighbor who knew the Marchbanks family. She's here for the tea and memories.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She overheard Lord Marchbanks mention a 'painting trick' and might share that clue if befriended. She also knows the grandson's situation.

npcs.name: The Art Collector
npcs.description: A bespectacled man in a tweed jacket, cataloguing artworks. He has no interest in Lot 47.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He's actually a forger hired by a rival to swap the painting if it becomes too hot. He'll only act if the bid exceeds £2,500.
